figures.caption: Figure 1: Imbalanced biregular obstruction motivating log-skewness. A large low-degree side A and a small high-degree side B create, after sampling, a dense core of load Ω(s).

Figure 2: Left: a rooted pattern, with edge colors indicating decomposition classes. Right: a sampled graph containing a tree of this pattern. Thick colored edges are greedy, and thin dotted edges are threshold. Black circles are left vertices and gray circles are right vertices. Each left vertex incident to a class-i greedy edge has at least αi class-i threshold edges.

Figure 3: Lower-bound construction for Greedy. The graph has layers V1, . . . , Vb with |Vi| = ti √ n, where t = (logn)3, and between consecutive layers the edges form ti disjoint copies of K√ n, √ nt. The figure shows the batch-wise propagation of load by Greedy: batch B1 pushes all of Vb−1 to load at least 1, batch B2 pushes all of Vb−2 to load at least 2, and so on.
